simt_parameters.threshold_time: A significant restoration of LTP (fEPSP slope increase ≥ 20% of baseline 60 min post-HFS, compared to <5% in aged controls) and a localized increase in neuronal lactate sensor FRET ratio (≥10% increase within 5 minutes post-HFS/optogenetic stimulation) in the optogenetically activated group, without significant changes in global perfusate lactate levels (less than 5% change from baseline), measured over a 2-hour experimental period. Power analysis for n=12 mice per group (aged control, aged ChR2+HFS, aged ChR2+HFS+light) to detect a 20% difference in LTP with 80% power and α=0.05.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: If optogenetic activation of astrocytic Ca2+ transients rescues both LTP and local lactate release, it strongly supports IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 (astrocytic precision failure) and argues against IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_02 (neuronal lactate uptake), IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_03 (ECM diffusion), and IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_05 (global lactate siphoning).

l6_tasks.title: Dose-Response and Temporal Specificity of Optogenetic Astrocyte Ca2+ Activation on Synaptic Plasticity and Local Metabolism

simt_parameters.system: Aged (18-24 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (400 µm thick, n=15 mice, 3 slices/mouse) with astrocyte-specific AAV5-GFAP-ChR2-eYFP expression, as described in T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_01. Slices are maintained in ACSF at 32°C.
simt_parameters.intervention: Optogenetic stimulation of astrocytic Ca2+ transients with varying parameters: Blue light (470 nm) delivered via fiber optic immediately following HFS (100 Hz for 1s, repeated 3 times at 20s intervals). Parameters to be varied: 1) Light intensity (5 mW/mm², 10 mW/mm², 20 mW/mm²), 2) Pulse duration (2ms, 5ms, 10ms), and 3) Frequency (5 Hz, 10 Hz, 20 Hz) for 30s. Control slices receive HFS only. A 3x3x3 factorial design will be employed to identify optimal parameters.
simt_parameters.meter: Electrophysiology: fEPSP slopes from CA1 pyramidal neurons before and 60 minutes after HFS. Local lactate sensing: FRET-based neuronal lactate sensor (Laconic) measured via two-photon microscopy in the stratum radiatum. Astrocyte Ca2+ imaging: GCaMP6f fluorescence to monitor Ca2+ transient amplitude and kinetics. Synaptic protein expression: Western blot analysis of PSD-95 and synaptophysin from micro-dissected CA1 regions 2 hours post-stimulation, with β-actin as loading control.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: Identification of specific optogenetic parameters (light intensity, pulse duration, frequency) that achieve ≥80% of young adult LTP levels (fEPSP slope increase ≥30% of baseline) and a corresponding ≥15% increase in local neuronal lactate FRET ratio within 5 minutes post-stimulation, with a dose-dependent relationship. This optimal parameter set should also prevent significant degradation of synaptic protein levels (less than 10% change from baseline) compared to HFS-only aged controls, measured over a 2-hour period. Statistical significance (ANOVA with post-hoc Tukey HSD, α=0.05) will be determined for each parameter combination.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment will identify the precise spatio-temporal requirements for astrocytic Ca2+ signaling to support synaptic plasticity and local metabolism, further strengthening IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 by demonstrating that not just any Ca2+ activation, but specific parameters, are effective. It also provides a foundation for future therapeutic interventions.

l6_tasks.title: Investigating the Role of Astrocyte-Neuron Lactate Shuttle Components Following Optogenetic Ca2+ Activation

simt_parameters.system: Aged (18-24 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (400 µm thick, n=10 mice, 3 slices/mouse) with astrocyte-specific AAV5-GFAP-ChR2-eYFP expression. Slices are maintained in ACSF at 32°C. Pharmacological inhibitors will be pre-applied.
simt_parameters.intervention: Optogenetic activation of astrocytic Ca2+ transients (using optimal parameters from T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02) immediately following HFS. This will be combined with pharmacological inhibition of key lactate shuttle components: 1) MCT1/2 inhibitor (e.g., α-cyano-4-hydroxycinnamate, CHC, 5 mM) to block lactate transport, 2) LDH inhibitor (e.g., Oxamate, 10 mM) to block lactate-pyruvate conversion. Each inhibitor will be applied for 30 minutes prior to HFS/optogenetic stimulation. Control groups include HFS+optogenetic stimulation without inhibitors, and HFS+inhibitors without optogenetic stimulation.
simt_parameters.meter: Electrophysiology: fEPSP slopes from CA1 pyramidal neurons before and 60 minutes after HFS. Local lactate sensing: FRET-based neuronal lactate sensor (Laconic) via two-photon microscopy. Astrocyte Ca2+ imaging: GCaMP6f fluorescence. Glycogen content: Enzymatic assay (e.g., Glycogen Assay Kit, Abcam ab65345) from micro-dissected CA1 regions 30 minutes post-stimulation, normalized to protein content.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: A significant attenuation (≥50% reduction) of the optogenetically-induced LTP rescue and local lactate increase by CHC and/or Oxamate, compared to the optogenetically-stimulated group without inhibitors, within the 2-hour experimental period. This suggests that the restored Ca2+ transients act upstream of these transporters/enzymes. Glycogen content should not be significantly altered (<10% change) by the optogenetic stimulation itself, indicating that the primary effect is on lactate dynamics rather than global energy stores. Statistical significance (ANOVA with post-hoc Tukey HSD, α=0.05) will be determined.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment will confirm that the restored astrocytic Ca2+ transients specifically influence the lactate shuttle mechanism. If inhibition of MCTs or LDH blocks the rescue effect, it further supports IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 by demonstrating the functional link between astrocytic Ca2+ and lactate metabolism, while also providing insight into the specific molecular targets involved.

l6_tasks.title: Comparison of Optogenetic Astrocyte Ca2+ Restoration vs. Exogenous Lactate Supplementation on LTP and Local Metabolism

simt_parameters.system: Aged (18-24 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (400 µm thick, n=12 mice, 3 slices/mouse) with astrocyte-specific AAV5-GFAP-ChR2-eYFP expression. Slices are maintained in ACSF at 32°C. Young (3-4 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (n=6 mice) will serve as a positive control for healthy LTP and lactate dynamics.
simt_parameters.intervention: Four experimental groups: 1) Aged HFS only, 2) Aged HFS + optogenetic astrocyte Ca2+ activation (optimal parameters from T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02), 3) Aged HFS + exogenous L-lactate (2 mM, Sigma L1750) added to ACSF 10 minutes prior to HFS and maintained throughout, 4) Young HFS only. The exogenous lactate concentration is chosen to mimic physiological levels during activity.
simt_parameters.meter: Electrophysiology: fEPSP slopes from CA1 pyramidal neurons before and 60 minutes after HFS. Local lactate sensing: FRET-based neuronal lactate sensor (Laconic) via two-photon microscopy. Astrocyte Ca2+ imaging: GCaMP6f fluorescence. Mitochondrial respiration: Oxygen consumption rate (OCR) in micro-dissected CA1 regions using Seahorse XF24 Analyzer, 30 minutes post-HFS, to assess neuronal metabolic state.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: If optogenetic Ca2+ activation restores LTP and local lactate to young levels (≥30% LTP, ≥15% local lactate increase), but exogenous lactate supplementation only partially rescues LTP (<15% LTP increase) or fails to restore local lactate kinetics to the same extent as optogenetic activation, it supports the precision failure of astrocytic Ca2+ (IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01) over a simple lactate deficit (IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_02). Mitochondrial respiration should show a significant increase (≥20%) in both optogenetically-rescued and young groups compared to aged HFS-only, indicating improved neuronal metabolic support. All measurements over a 2-hour period. Statistical significance will be determined by ANOVA with post-hoc comparisons (α=0.05).

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment directly compares the efficacy of targeted astrocytic Ca2+ restoration with general lactate supplementation. If the former is more effective or precise, it strengthens IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 by demonstrating that the issue is not merely a lack of lactate, but a failure in its activity-dependent, localized delivery.

l6_tasks.title: Investigating the Effect of Global Astrocyte Activation on LTP and Lactate Dynamics in Aged Slices

simt_parameters.system: Aged (18-24 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (400 µm thick, n=12 mice, 3 slices/mouse) with astrocyte-specific AAV5-GFAP-hM3Dq expression. Slices are maintained in ACSF at 32°C. Young (3-4 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (n=6 mice) will serve as a positive control.
simt_parameters.intervention: Four experimental groups: 1) Aged HFS only, 2) Aged HFS + CNO (10 µM) applied globally to activate hM3Dq-expressing astrocytes, 3) Aged HFS + optogenetic astrocyte Ca2+ activation (optimal parameters from T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02, for comparison), 4) Young HFS only. CNO will be applied 10 minutes prior to HFS and maintained. The global CNO activation will induce widespread astrocytic Ca2+ increases, contrasting with the localized optogenetic approach.
simt_parameters.meter: Electrophysiology: fEPSP slopes from CA1 pyramidal neurons before and 60 minutes after HFS. Local lactate sensing: FRET-based neuronal lactate sensor (Laconic) via two-photon microscopy. Astrocyte Ca2+ imaging: GCaMP6f fluorescence (co-expressed with hM3Dq) to confirm global Ca2+ increases. Synaptic ultrastructure: Electron microscopy analysis of synapse density and morphology in the CA1 stratum radiatum 2 hours post-stimulation.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: If global astrocytic activation by CNO fails to fully rescue LTP (e.g., <15% LTP increase) and local lactate dynamics, or leads to non-specific changes compared to the targeted optogenetic activation, it supports the importance of perisynaptic precision (IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01). If global activation leads to a significant increase in global perfusate lactate (≥10% increase) without proportional local neuronal lactate increase, it would suggest a siphoning effect (IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_05). Synapse density and morphology should be preserved in the optogenetically-rescued group, but potentially altered in the globally activated group if non-specific effects occur. All measurements over a 2-hour period. Statistical significance will be determined by ANOVA with post-hoc comparisons (α=0.05).

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment will differentiate between the need for precise, localized astrocytic Ca2+ signaling (IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01) and a more general need for astrocytic activation or lactate supply (IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_05). If global activation is less effective or has detrimental side effects, it highlights the importance of spatial precision.

l6_tasks.title: Transcriptomic and Proteomic Analysis of Astrocyte-Neuron Signaling Pathways Following Optogenetic Ca2+ Restoration

simt_parameters.system: Aged (18-24 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (400 µm thick, n=18 mice, 3 slices/mouse) with astrocyte-specific AAV5-GFAP-ChR2-eYFP expression. Slices are maintained in ACSF at 32°C. Young (3-4 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (n=6 mice) will serve as a positive control.
simt_parameters.intervention: Three experimental groups: 1) Aged HFS only, 2) Aged HFS + optogenetic astrocyte Ca2+ activation (optimal parameters from T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02), 3) Young HFS only. Slices will be harvested at 30 minutes and 2 hours post-stimulation for molecular analysis.
simt_parameters.meter: RNA sequencing (RNA-seq): Bulk RNA-seq from micro-dissected CA1 regions (n=6 biological replicates per group per timepoint) to identify differentially expressed genes (DEGs) related to metabolism, synaptic function, and astrocyte-neuron communication. Proteomics: Targeted mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) on micro-dissected CA1 regions (n=6 biological replicates per group per timepoint) focusing on proteins involved in glycolysis, lactate transport (MCTs), mitochondrial function, and synaptic plasticity (e.g., PSD-95, GluA1). Data will be analyzed for pathway enrichment (e.g., GO, KEGG) and correlated with LTP and lactate measurements.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: Identification of a distinct set of DEGs (FDR < 0.05, |log2FC| > 1.5) and differentially abundant proteins (p < 0.05, fold change > 1.2) in the optogenetically-rescued aged group that are significantly enriched in pathways related to lactate metabolism, mitochondrial biogenesis, and synaptic plasticity, and that are restored towards young levels. This should occur within 2 hours post-stimulation. The identified changes should correlate with the observed rescue of LTP and local lactate dynamics.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This multi-omics approach will provide a comprehensive molecular signature of the rescue mechanism, identifying key genes and proteins regulated by astrocytic Ca2+ transients. This directly supports IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01 by detailing the molecular machinery involved in the precision failure and its restoration.

l6_tasks.title: Real-time Metabolic Flux Analysis in Astrocytes and Neurons Following Optogenetic Ca2+ Restoration

simt_parameters.system: Aged (18-24 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (400 µm thick, n=12 mice, 3 slices/mouse) with astrocyte-specific AAV5-GFAP-ChR2-eYFP and neuron-specific AAV5-CaMKII-Laconic expression. Slices are maintained in ACSF at 32°C. Young (3-4 months) C57BL/6J mouse hippocampal slices (n=6 mice) will serve as a positive control.
simt_parameters.intervention: Three experimental groups: 1) Aged HFS only, 2) Aged HFS + optogenetic astrocyte Ca2+ activation (optimal parameters from T_L6_M_G1_1_01_A_02), 3) Young HFS only. Metabolic inhibitors (e.g., 2-deoxyglucose for glycolysis, rotenone for mitochondrial complex I) will be used to probe metabolic pathways.
simt_parameters.meter: Real-time metabolic flux: Two-photon imaging of genetically encoded sensors for ATP (e.g., AT1.03), NADH (e.g., Peredox), and pyruvate (e.g., Pyronic) in both astrocytes and neurons, simultaneously with neuronal lactate sensor (Laconic) and astrocytic GCaMP6f. Measurements will be taken before, during, and after HFS and optogenetic stimulation. Electrophysiology: fEPSP slopes from CA1 pyramidal neurons. Glucose uptake: Fluorescent glucose analog 2-NBDG uptake in astrocytes and neurons via live imaging.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: A significant increase (≥10%) in astrocytic glycolysis (e.g., increased NADH/ATP ratio, increased 2-NBDG uptake) and subsequent neuronal pyruvate/ATP levels (≥10% increase) in the optogenetically-rescued aged group, mirroring young controls, within 15 minutes post-stimulation. This should correlate with restored LTP (fEPSP slope increase ≥20%). Inhibition of glycolysis should abolish these effects. The kinetic profile of these metabolic changes should be tightly coupled to the Ca2+ transients and localized lactate release, confirming the metabolic link. Statistical significance (ANOVA with post-hoc Tukey HSD, α=0.05) will be determined.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment will provide direct, real-time evidence of how astrocytic Ca2+ transients influence metabolic flux in both astrocytes and neurons, confirming the functional role of the astrocyte-neuron lactate shuttle in supporting synaptic plasticity. This directly elucidates the mechanism underlying IH_Q_L3_M_G1_1_01.
